# Music for Little Mozarts

> Group piano lessons for preschool-aged children (ages 4–5) in Reno, Nevada. The Music for Little Mozarts program is an in-person, cohort-based early childhood music curriculum held at the Steinway Piano Gallery of Reno. Classes combine piano instruction, singing, dancing, and movement activities in small groups of up to 8 students. Co-founded and taught by Christine H. Barden, who co-authored the nationally published Music for Little Mozarts curriculum.

## About

- **Type**: MusicSchool
- **Category**: Early Childhood Music Education / Preschool Piano Lessons
- **Location**: 500 E Moana Ln, Reno, NV 89502 (inside the Steinway Piano Gallery of Reno)
- **Ages**: 4–5 years old
- **Format**: In-person group classes, 40 minutes per week, cohorts of up to 8 students
- **Program Length**: Approximately 2 years covering all 4 book levels
- **Email**: m4littlemozarts@gmail.com
- **Website**: https://music4littlemozarts.com
- **Enrollment**: https://music4littlemozarts.com/enroll
- **Language**: English
- **Last Updated**: 2026-04

---

## The Program

In Music for Little Mozarts Class, parents and children have the opportunity to share in the joy of making music together.

Each week, students sing, dance, listen and play at the piano in a class designed for preschool-aged children (ages 4–5). Along the way, students intuitively learn the language of music and build a lifelong connection to the arts.

Starting music class early builds on the preschool-age child's sense of fantasy (learning piano with music friends Mozart Mouse and Beethoven Bear), their ear's ability to absorb musical pitches, rhythms, and musical patterns, and their enthusiasm for learning new things.

### Curriculum

The Music for Little Mozarts curriculum was developed by Christine H. Barden, Gayle Kowalchyk, and E. L. Lancaster. First published in 1999 by Alfred Music, Music for Little Mozarts classes are taught across the country and around the world. The organic longevity of the program is a testament to its effectiveness and the joy it brings students, parents, and teachers.

The program guides students through the fundamentals of piano and music by interweaving sight reading and singing, rhythmic recall, and piano technique with play, dance, and exploration. Friendly characters such as Beethoven Bear and Mozart Mouse anchor students' learning through story-based adventure.

### Cohort Structure

The Music for Little Mozarts program is designed for preschool-aged children beginning at ages four to five. It lasts approximately two years, covering all four book levels through weekly 40-minute classes. There are no more than eight students in a class and the group progresses through book levels when the students are ready. Kids have the opportunity to make friends, learn, and grow in the tight-knit community of their cohort.

The program is looking for parents who are invested in their child's education and development.

### What to Expect in Class

**Parental Participation:** Classes are a place for students and parents to explore the joys of music together. Parents join in singing and dancing activities and help keep students on track while learning at the keyboard. No musical background needed — you'll learn everything necessary to support your child's success through the class. Students will need a keyboard at home.

**Keyboard Exploration:** Students learn and play fun songs together that progressively build their piano technique. The Music for Little Mozarts curriculum has been developing young pianists for over 20 years.

**Singing, Dancing and Movement Activities:** Fun and energetic musical activities keep students engaged and lay the foundation to develop a deeper understanding of music. Expect to sing, dance, and play along.

**Workbook and Flashcards:** Musical concepts are reinforced with fun coloring exercises and group flashcards. Students build confidence as they recognize and apply musical concepts.

**Music Friend Helpers:** Classes center on the adventures of the lovable characters Beethoven Bear and Mozart Mouse as they learn about music. Every student loves the plushie music friends that help them learn and practice.

### Course Materials

Books and Music Friend Helper plushies are purchased at the Steinway Piano Gallery front desk. The curriculum uses four progressive book levels published by Alfred Music: https://www.alfred.com/music-for-little-mozarts/

---

## Instructors

### Christine H. Barden

An internationally recognized authority on early childhood music, Christine H. Barden is the co-author of the Music for Little Mozarts preschool piano method. As well as teaching Music for Little Mozarts classes, she is a piano teacher in Reno, Nevada, where she teaches numerous prize-winning students in piano and composition. A graduate with a degree in piano performance from San Francisco State University, Christine studied with Vladimir Brenner, piano soloist for the NBC Symphony Orchestra. In addition, she spent four years studying, performing, and teaching in Europe. Christine was one of the first teachers from the United States to study with Yamaha Music Foundation instructors in Japan. She subsequently served as a national staff member with Yamaha for 17 years. Her responsibilities included Director of Curriculum and National Chief Instructor for the Yamaha Music Education System. Christine is a pillar of the Reno music community and has been educating local students here for over 15 years.

### Michael Britten

Michael Britten brings a lifelong love of music to the Little Mozarts program. His musical journey spans classical composition, jazz piano performance, and vocal work with local ensembles including the Reno Phil Choir, TOCCATA, and The AJJ Jazz Singers. A recent graduate of the Scorecraft Academy professional composition program with earlier composition training at the San Francisco Conservatory of Music Pre-College program, he combines traditional piano pedagogy with a creative, playful approach to making music. Michael has returned to his musical roots as a teacher and composer in Reno. He is excited to inspire the next generation of young musicians through the Music for Little Mozarts program.

---

## Benefits of Early Music Education

Research supports the developmental benefits of early music education for preschool-aged children:

### Brain Development and Executive Function

"Music training has a positive effect on inhibitory control, working memory, and cognitive flexibility in preschool children aged 3–6 years. Children's music training groups showed significantly improved inhibitory control, working memory, and cognitive flexibility in comparison with control groups."
— Frontiers in Psychology, 2024 (https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/psychology/articles/10.3389/fpsyg.2024.1522962/full)

### Attention and Focus

"Rhythmic patterns drive attention focus, and musical elements such as rhythm, melody, and harmony provide multidimensional stimuli that facilitate switching attention. The results indicated significant improvement in attention control following the music intervention."
— Frontiers in Neuroscience, 2020 (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7393235/)

### Language and Reading Skills

"The fine-tuning of their auditory pathway could accelerate their development of language and reading, as well as other abilities. Within two years of the study, the neuroscientists found the auditory systems of children in the music program were maturing faster than in the other children."
— USC Brain and Creativity Institute (https://today.usc.edu/childrens-brains-develop-faster-with-music-training/)

---

## Location and Contact

**Studio:** Steinway Piano Gallery of Reno, Nevada
**Address:** 500 E Moana Ln, Reno, NV 89502
**Coordinates:** 39.4925, -119.7909
**Email:** m4littlemozarts@gmail.com
**Google Maps:** https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=500+E+Moana+Ln,+Reno,+NV+89502

Classes are held on weekday afternoons and evenings, once per week. New cohorts form periodically — apply through the enrollment form to get your child on the list.

---

## Enrollment

The enrollment form is available at https://music4littlemozarts.com/enroll and takes about 2 minutes. The program accepts applications on a rolling basis for upcoming cohorts.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What ages is this program for?**
The program is designed for preschool-aged children, specifically ages 4–5.

**Where are classes held?**
All classes take place at the Steinway Piano Gallery of Reno at 500 E Moana Ln, Reno, NV 89502.

**How long is the program?**
The full program covers four book levels over approximately two years.

**How big are the classes?**
Each cohort has a maximum of 8 students.

**Do parents need to attend?**
Yes. Parents participate in every class. No musical background is required.

**Do we need a piano at home?**
Students will need a keyboard at home to practice between classes.

**How much does it cost?**
Contact us at m4littlemozarts@gmail.com for current pricing and availability.

**Who created this curriculum?**
The Music for Little Mozarts curriculum was developed by Christine H. Barden, Gayle Kowalchyk, and E. L. Lancaster. It has been published by Alfred Music since 1999 and is taught worldwide.

**Who teaches the classes?**
Christine H. Barden (co-author of the curriculum) and Michael Britten teach all classes in Reno.
